title: Menstrual health & hygiene (MHH) unit
context: Menstrual health and hygiene (MHH) is a core WASH domain topic that is frequently neglected in resource-constrained settings. MHH content requires particular care for cultural appropriateness, gender sensitivity, and dignity — guidance that is technically correct but stigmatizing or inapt is not shippable. This unit builds on the authoring pattern from content-006 and requires both WASH accuracy review and dedicated cultural/dignity review.
objective: Author, source-cite, and get both WASH domain and cultural/dignity review of a WashUnit document covering menstrual health and hygiene: hygienic management during menstruation (materials, cleaning/disposal), access to water and sanitation facilities, and dignity/privacy considerations — at the household-practice level, within scope (no clinical instruction).
acceptanceCriteria: Every claim tied to a specific named authoritative source (WHO/UNICEF/Sphere) with retrieval date and verified license; no verbatim copied text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
Unit conforms to the WashUnit schema: all required fields present, riskClass is medium, no clinical instruction, steps are plain-language per the style guide
Unit reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author for accuracy and scope compliance

Unit additionally reviewed by a cultural/dignity reviewer for gender-sensitive, non-stigmatizing, and dignity-preserving framing that is appropriate for the target communities; both reviewers distinct from the author
PR-tied review-log entries written for both the accuracy review and the cultural/dignity review
CI schema validation, citation-coverage, no-self-approval, readability, and copy/similarity gates all pass
output: A committed, domain-reviewed and cultural/dignity-reviewed, source-cited WashUnit document covering menstrual health and hygiene at the household-practice level, with PR-tied review-log entries.
